paragraphs.text: Firstly, I would like to say that I completely support this act and hope it fixes the massive issue of video games with gambling mechanics that require you to purchase arbitrary in-game currency. These predatory mechanics only lead to massive gambling addiction for adults as well as children. These evil companies have been profiting from misery and addiction, hiring phycologists to make their predatory systems as addictive as possible to encourage and keep people spending money. I hope that games with these predatory monetization schemes get highly regulated and it would be no longer possible to use real money and gamble in video games, I would fully support to outlaw intermediate currency in all video games. Either have points fully earnable in-game or only bought with actual money, instead of "gems" that you have to buy first and only then can purchase the thing you want, they do this do obscure the actual amount of money you spend, exactly like casino chips. I have family a member that has been made a gambling addict as a result of these evil practices so I have no sympathy for these companies. I also believe that this act could address the issue of video game/digital ownership as outlined by the Stop Destroying Videogames citizens initiative as companies are effectively taking away purchased goods after a sale has been made.
